test: A/B Test — Groq Baseline vs QLoRA

results.weak: Did Python programming and made some scripts
results.reference: Developed Python automation scripts reducing manual work by 60%
results.groq_output: * Designed and developed 15+ Python scripts, leveraging libraries such as NumPy and Pandas, to automate data processing tasks and increase efficiency by 30% within a 6-month period.
results.qlora_note: QLoRA model requires GPU inference — evaluated qualitatively on Colab

results.weak: Did network setup and IT support for company
results.reference: Configured Cisco routers for 500+ user enterprise network maintaining 99.9% uptime
results.groq_output: * Designed, implemented, and managed network infrastructure for 500+ employees, resulting in 99.9% uptime and a 40% reduction in IT support requests, utilizing expertise in TCP/IP, DNS, and Active Directory to ensure seamless system operations and enhance overall network security and performance.
results.qlora_note: QLoRA model requires GPU inference — evaluated qualitatively on Colab

results.weak: Worked on data analysis and made reports
results.reference: Analyzed 1M+ records using Python/Pandas, delivering insights that increased revenue by 25%
results.groq_output: * Utilized data analysis techniques to drive business insights, generating 25+ detailed reports per quarter that informed strategic decision-making and resulted in a 30% increase in data-driven initiatives.
results.qlora_note: QLoRA model requires GPU inference — evaluated qualitatively on Colab

results.reference: Resolved 50+ daily support tickets maintaining 95% customer satisfaction rate
results.groq_output: * Resolved an average of 25 customer complaints per week, achieving a 95% customer satisfaction rate and reducing issue escalation by 30% through effective problem-solving and communication skills.
results.qlora_note: QLoRA model requires GPU inference — evaluated qualitatively on Colab

summary.recommendation: Use Groq for production (better quality). Use QLoRA for offline/edge deployment.
